para.preformatted: Ver Who Date Changes - ----- -----------------------------------------------. 1.00 Nishant 19/12/19 Added support for vck190, VCU118 DpTxSs_VideoPhyInit() renamed to DpTxSs_PhyInit() set_vphy() renamed to config_phy() and has two parameters for linerate and lanecount. The application files are common for ZCU102, VCU118 and VCK190 TX Only design 1.01 KU 22/10/20 Added support for fabric 8b10b implementation of of DP1.4 1.02 ND 01/12/21 Added support for VSC in application menu. Changed options for Format menu. 1.03 ND 04/01/21 Moved all global variables declaration from .h to .c files due to gcc compiler compilation error. Setting tx_is_reconnected=0 and setting max_cap_new=XDP_TX_LINK_BW_SET_810GBPS in hpd_con for TX CTS test case. 1.04 KU 04/12/21 Updated Versal GT programming to get /20 clk 1.05 ND 05/07/21 Setting bit[12] of Reg 0x1A4 to 1 for VSC Colorimetry support 1.06 ND 07/21/22 Updated the LMK03318 address. 1.07 ND 08/11/22 Added support for gpio to monitor gtpowergood for versal GT 1.08 ND 08/26/22 Added DELAY macro to increase delay in IDT_8T49N24x_SetClock() if encountering iic race condition.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 Nishant 19/12/20 Added support for vck190.Added ReadModifyWrite() for writing to vck190 register space.set_vphy() renamed to config_phy() and has two parameters for linerate and lanecount.

briefdescription.para: This function is the main entry point for the interrupt example using the XDpTxSs driver.

detaileddescription.para: This function will set up the system with interrupts and set up Hot-Plug-Event (HPD) handlers.

parameterdescription.para: is the unique device ID of the DisplayPort TX Subsystem core.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if the system setup failed.
XST_SUCCESS should never return since this function, if setup was successful, is blocking.

simplesect.para: If system setup was successful, this function is blocking in order to illustrate interrupt handling taking place for HPD events.

briefdescription.para: This function initialize required platform-specifc peripherals.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if required peripherals are initialized and configured successfully.

briefdescription.para: This function takes care HPD event.
This function takes care HPD pulse interrupt.
This function sets link line rate.
This function sets up the interrupt system so interrupts can occur for the DisplayPort TX Subsystem core.

detaileddescription.para: The function is application-specific since the actual system may or may not have an interrupt controller. The DPTX Subsystem core could be directly connected to a processor without an interrupt controller. The user should modify this function to fit the application.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if interrupt setup was successful.
A specific error code defined in "xstatus.h" if an error occurs.

briefdescription.para: This function configures Video Phy.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if Video Phy configured successfully.

briefdescription.para: This function use h/w timer to count specific Microseconds.

detaileddescription.para:
pointer
to timer
MicroSeconds
to wait
None.
None. This function use h/w timer to count specific Microseconds.
It provides timer with predefined amount of loop iterations.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

simplesect.para: Use the XDpTxSs_SetUserTimerHandler driver function to set this function as the handler custom delay/sleep.

briefdescription.para: This function sets up DPTxSubsystem.
This function sets proper ref clk frequency and line rate.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Video PHY instance.

briefdescription.para: This function sets GT in 16-bits (2-Byte) or 32-bits (4-Byte) mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Video PHY instance.

briefdescription.para: This function to check MMCM lock status.
This function is the main hpd pulse process.
This function updates required timing values in Video Pattern Generator core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.
specifies vertical split mode to be used for splitting resolution vertically.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if Video pattern configured successfully.

briefdescription.para: This function sets user pattern.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

briefdescription.para: This function to calculate EDID checksum.
This function to find out preferred Video Mode ID.
This function reports CRC values of Video components.
This is the main function for XDpTxSs interrupt example.

detaileddescription.para: If the DpTxSs_IntrExample function which sets up the system succeeds, this function will wait for the interrupts. Once a connection event or pulse is detected, DpTxSs will RX device capabilities and re-start the subsystem.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if the interrupt example was unsuccessful.

simplesect.para: Unless setup failed, main will never return since DpTxSs_IntrExample is blocking (it is waiting on interrupts for Hot-Plug-Detect (HPD) events.

memberdef.argsstring: (u32 I2CBaseAddress, u8 I2CSlaveAddress, u16 RegisterAddress, u8 Value)
memberdef.name: i2c_write_dp141

briefdescription.para: This function sets up PHY.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if interrupt setup was successful.
A specific error code defined in "xstatus.h" if an error occurs.

briefdescription.para: This function starts tx process.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if interrupt setup was successful.
A specific error code defined in "xstatus.h" if an error occurs.

memberdef.argsstring: (XDpTxSs *InstancePtr, u8 Edid_org[128], u8 Edid1_org[128], u16 res_update)
memberdef.name: hpd_con

briefdescription.para: This function is called when a Hot-Plug-Detect (HPD) event is received by the DisplayPort TX Subsystem core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs instance.

simplesect.para: Use the XDpTxSs_SetCallback driver function to set this function as the handler for HPD event.

briefdescription.para: This function powers down sink.
This function powers down sink.
This function returns current line rate.
This function returns current lane counts.
This function sets VPHY based on the linerate.
This function power cycle the sink.
This function will detect video resolution/frequency change Many GPUs won't re-train when only video res/freq change.

detaileddescription.para: Application needs to know if it changed and need to detect new parameter. Based on the parameter, re-set and re-start VDAM and Tx
None This function will detect video resolution/frequency change Many GPUs won't re-train when only video res/freq change.

briefdescription.para: This function scans VFMC- IIC.
This function reads DP141 VFMC- IIC.
